bot_answer: Use the Staff Directory page for Oxford Miami University Libraries [1]. You can find it here: https://www.lib.miamioh.edu/about/organization/staff/ [1].

Interlibrary Loan for Hamilton Campus is handled through Rentschler Library, and you submit the request yourself [1]. Use the ILL page for the request process: https://libguides.lib.miamioh.edu/ILL [1].
Yes — Middletown ILL pickup is at Gardner-Harvey [1][2]. For submitting the request, use the Interlibrary Loan page for Middletown Campus (Gardner-Harvey Library) [6].
I don't have a reliable answer to that. You can ask a librarian directly through Ask Us.

Miami University Libraries provides New York Times access for Miami affiliated users [2][7]. To access it, use the New York Times subscription access link from the Libraries’ Databases A–Z page [1][6].

Yes. Miami University Libraries sponsors Wall Street Journal accounts for current students, faculty, and staff, and access works from any location, on or off campus [1][3]. To activate it, use the WSJ partner registration page and sign up with your Miami email [3].

Yes — Miami University Libraries has a citation LibGuide at https://libguides.lib.miamioh.edu/citation [1]. The library also has a shortcut URL, https://www.lib.miamioh.edu/citing, that redirects to the same guide [2].

For help with your dissertation literature review, use the Miami University Libraries “Ask Us” page to connect with a librarian via chat/email/phone or to set up an appointment/research consultation [1][5]. If you want to work with a subject specialist, use the Subject Liaison (librarian) directory to find the best contact for your discipline [3][4].
